body.title: Intent extractor: backfill tickets + decoration hook at add time
body.result: cc_queue.py decoration hook + intent_backfill.py — predict on add, validate on add with existing intention, backfill script for existing tickets; fail-open on device unavailability
body.intention: I intend that every ticket has an inferred intention pre-stamped at add time and that the hook fails open when the intent device is unavailable — cc_queue.py add is never blocked by the intent extractor.
body.description:
Two integration points for the intent extractor in the ticket pipeline:

1. Decoration hook at cc_queue.py add time: call predict(context=ticket_description, domain=coding) and stamp inferred_intention into ticket metadata if intention field is blank. If intention field already has a value, call validate() with it as actual_outcome — every manually-authored intention is a free validation signal. MUST fail open: if intent extractor device is unavailable, add proceeds normally with a warning (same pattern as Scraps offline).

2. Backfill script: one-time run over existing tickets missing intention field. Calls predict() for each. Calls validate() for closed tickets using result field as actual_outcome.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (decoration hook); lab/claudecode/intent_backfill.py (new one-shot script)
**Design rules:** external-state; idempotent; no SQLite; fail-open on device unavailability
**Scope boundary:** IN — decoration hook; backfill script; graceful degradation. OUT — predict() logic changes.
**Completion criteria:**
  1. cc_queue.py add stamps inferred_intention in ticket metadata when intent field is blank
  2. cc_queue.py add proceeds normally when intent extractor device is unreachable (warning printed, no exception raised)
  3. Backfill script produces plausible intentions on 5 sample tickets
  4. Closed tickets with result field generate validate() calls — verifiable via devlab.validations count increase

CC.0 acked at 2026-06-15T20:51:16Z
